$comment: Architectural fitness rules for codewatch. Run with `codewatch graph check`. Thresholds chosen against the post-M20 baseline. M26: glob excludes replaced with role-based excludes — a single `excludeRoles: ["test", "fixture"]` is more semantic than `**/__tests__/**` and survives a directory rename.

rules.$comment: Tightened from 500 → 350 in M27 because baseline mode lets us ratchet without breaking existing files. New files >350 fail; the 3 carryovers (template.ts 377, check.ts 375, index.ts 351) stay flagged but don't block.

C-14: `entry` joins the exclude set — a shebang-prefixed executable entry (e.g. the CLI's index.ts) legitimately wires up many command modules, the same high-fan-out-is-fine rationale as a re-export barrel. C-62: graph-cli.ts is the `graph`-subcommand composition root — the same aggregator rationale, but it has no shebang so the classifier doesn't label it `entry`; excluded by path. Bucketing its registrars into arbitrary group files would be indirection existing only to dodge this threshold, so an explicit exempt for the honest fan-out is preferred.

Replaces three former forbid-import rules (render→cli, graph→cli, core→analyzer) with one declarative layer spec. New packages added to the wrong layer fail automatically without anyone remembering to add a rule. Layer strings are path prefixes — longest-prefix-match wins, consistent with no-internal-only-barrels.packageRoots. Previously written as bare names like "core" but those silently matched nothing on real codewatch node ids (packages/core/src/…); fixed in C-15.

Textbook scary-hotspot signal: high churn × high cognitive load = the riskiest file to touch. Promoted to error in M27 — baseline mode means existing hotspots are carryover, so this only fails PRs that *worsen* the count. Switched from cyclomatic_max to cognitive_max (Sonarsource canon, M39) — cognitive scores nesting and branching realistically and tends higher than cyclomatic, so the threshold tracks alongside. The recency_30d factor (min(1, file_age_days/30), C-41) discounts freshly-authored files whose churn is initial authoring, not decay — a file younger than the window scales down proportionally, an older one is unaffected (factor 1). Run with --baseline main in CI; without baseline this rule will fail on existing files.

Flags internal sub-package barrels (role=barrel files with zero importers from outside their package). Internal barrels add an indirection without abstraction value and obscure the partition-quality picture by re-attributing edges. Public-API barrels (one per package, imported across package boundaries) are kept. Promoted from warning to error after #24 cleared all 13 carryover violations — only the public-API barrels remain, so any new internal barrel fails CI immediately. C-14: the former `exclude` of packages/cli/src/index.ts is gone — the role classifier now detects its `#!` shebang and labels it `entry` (a first-class executable-entry role, not re-export plumbing), so it never reaches this barrel-only rule.
